data.source: OpenChargeMap API daily charger location counts by state/region

behavior: Rapid week-over-week growth in publicly available EV charging locations signals infrastructure investment momentum and policy acceleration, typically preceding EV sales surges.

market_impact.rationale: Industrial manufacturing and installation of charging infrastructure drives equipment, construction, and logistics demand.

trade_logic.entry: If weekly net new charger locations exceed 200-unit threshold above trailing 4-week average
trade_logic.exit: Exit after 10 trading days or if growth falls below 150-unit weekly pace

reason_it_will_fire_in_30_days: EV infrastructure deployment is steady and continuous; weekly threshold breaches occur multiple times per quarter.

data.source: USGS earthquake API daily magnitude 4.0+ events by region with historical comparison

behavior: Clusters of moderate earthquakes in populated regions trigger immediate insurance claim filings and property damage estimates, creating short-term sector rotation into insurance and repair stocks.

market_impact.rationale: Insurance and reinsurance companies see claim volume spikes; financial sector benefits from short-term repricing of risk premiums.

trade_logic.entry: If 3+ magnitude 4.0+ earthquakes occur within 7 days in same region (e.g., California, Pacific Northwest)
trade_logic.exit: Exit after 8 trading days or if no additional earthquakes occur in next 5 days

reason_it_will_fire_in_30_days: Seismic activity is unpredictable but frequent enough that multi-event clusters occur several times per year in active zones.

data.source: Open-Meteo daily temperature and humidity for major US metros; compare to 10-year seasonal norms

behavior: Extreme heat or cold days significantly above/below seasonal average drive peak electricity and natural gas demand, improving utility operating margins and signaling commodity pressure.

market_impact.rationale: Peak demand days increase revenue and earnings potential for utility operators; commodity hedges also improve pricing power.

trade_logic.entry: If temperature deviation exceeds +15°F or -15°F from 10-year daily average in 3+ major metros simultaneously
trade_logic.exit: Exit after 5 trading days or when temperatures normalize within ±5°F of seasonal average

reason_it_will_fire_in_30_days: Seasonal temperature extremes occur regularly; winter and summer spike seasons guarantee multiple triggering events per month.

data.source: FRED series UNRATE (U.S. unemployment rate) weekly; compare to consensus forecast and prior month

behavior: When actual unemployment releases beat consensus expectations by more than 0.2%, it signals stronger labor demand and consumer cash flow, driving discretionary spending optimism.

market_impact.rationale: Better-than-expected jobs data lowers recession fears and supports consumer confidence, directly benefiting discretionary retailers and services.

trade_logic.entry: If reported UNRATE is ≥0.2% lower than consensus forecast and prior month reading
trade_logic.exit: Exit after 7 trading days or if next week's jobless claims spike above 5-week average

reason_it_will_fire_in_30_days: Monthly employment reports provide weekly trigger windows; surprise beats happen 3–4 times per year.

idea: Google Trends Surge in Luxury Good Searches

data.source: Google Trends weekly search volume for keywords: 'luxury watches', 'designer handbags', 'premium jewelry' vs. baseline

behavior: Spikes in luxury good search interest often precede discretionary spending waves and signal consumer confidence inflection, especially around holiday seasons and bonus periods.

market_impact.rationale: Luxury search spikes correlate with consumer optimism and willingness to spend on premium goods, directly driving discretionary sector valuations.

trade_logic.entry: If luxury goods search index exceeds 70 (on 0-100 scale) and rises >15 points week-over-week
trade_logic.exit: Exit after 10 trading days or if index drops below 55

reason_it_will_fire_in_30_days: Luxury search cycles align with retail calendars; Q4 and bonus season trigger multiple spikes annually.

idea: RSS News Volume Spike on Food Supply Chain Disruptions

data.source: RSS feed count aggregation for keywords: 'food supply', 'crop failure', 'transportation delay' from logistics/agriculture news feeds; weekly volume comparison

behavior: Spikes in food supply chain disruption news coverage signal pricing pressure and logistics cost inflation, typically benefiting transportation and energy stocks while pressuring consumer staples margins.

market_impact.rationale: Food supply disruptions compress staples margins and force price increases that reduce consumer purchasing power.

trade_logic.entry: If weekly RSS article count for supply chain keywords exceeds 50-article threshold and is >30% above 4-week average
trade_logic.exit: Exit after 8 trading days or if article volume returns below 35-article weekly run rate

reason_it_will_fire_in_30_days: Agricultural and logistics disruptions occur seasonally; weather, pest outbreaks, and transport strikes trigger multiple events per quarter.

idea: Tech Sector Volatility Crush Reversal Play

data.source: Yahoo Finance daily close prices and intraday range for XLK (technology ETF); calculate 20-day realized volatility vs. 100-day baseline

behavior: When tech sector volatility falls to 30% below its 100-day average, it often signals complacency that precedes a sharp volatility re-expansion and repricing event.

market_impact.rationale: Low volatility in tech often precedes correction periods as valuations become stretched and sentiment turns; positioning unwinds create sharp drawdowns.

trade_logic.entry: If XLK 20-day realized volatility drops below 70% of 100-day baseline and closes near daily high
trade_logic.exit: Exit after 10 trading days or once volatility spikes above 100-day baseline

reason_it_will_fire_in_30_days: Tech volatility cycles are frequent and predictable; complacency periods occur several times per quarter.

idea: Bank Equity vs. Bond Spread Inversion Signal

data.source: Yahoo Finance daily prices for XLF (financials ETF) and TLT (20-year Treasury ETF); calculate relative performance and yield curve proxy

behavior: When bank stocks dramatically outperform long-duration bonds while yield curve steepens, it signals inflation expectations rising and aggressive rate hike pricing that pressures financial system margins.

market_impact.rationale: Rapid bond selloffs combined with bank rallies often precede forced deleveraging as refinancing costs spike and credit spreads widen.

trade_logic.entry: If XLF outperforms TLT by >3% over 5 trading days and TLT closes down >1.5% on the day
trade_logic.exit: Exit after 7 trading days or if performance spread narrows back to <1% over 3-day window

reason_it_will_fire_in_30_days: Rate volatility spikes occur regularly; yield curve dynamics shift frequently enough to trigger entry conditions multiple times per month.

data.source: FRED series DCOILWTICO (crude oil prices) and GOLDAMND (gold spot) daily; calculate 10-day drawdown from 20-day high

behavior: When both crude oil and gold drawdown sharply (>8% over 10 days) simultaneously, it signals macro risk-off sentiment crushing commodities-linked valuations, often creating mean-reversion bounce opportunities in materials stocks.

market_impact.rationale: Extreme commodity weakness creates valuation compression in materials and mining stocks, generating technical bounce setups as oversold conditions reverse.

trade_logic.entry: If both crude and gold fall >8% from 20-day highs within 10-day window and XLB closes at 20-day low
trade_logic.exit: Exit after 6 trading days or if either commodity bounces >3% from entry low

reason_it_will_fire_in_30_days: Commodity volatility is consistent; major drawdown events occur 3–4 times per year, providing regular entry opportunities.

idea: Healthcare Cost Google Trends Acceleration Index

data.source: Google Trends weekly search volume for 'medical bill debt', 'prescription cost', 'health insurance claim denial'; calculate acceleration week-over-week

behavior: Sharp spikes in healthcare cost-related searches signal consumer stress and inability to afford medical services, often preceding healthcare system margin compression and policy-driven sector rotation.

market_impact.rationale: Rising healthcare cost searches correlate with consumer financial distress, increasing defaults on medical debt and forcing payers to tighten reimbursement rates.

trade_logic.entry: If combined search volume index rises >25% week-over-week and exceeds 60 on 0-100 scale
trade_logic.exit: Exit after 9 trading days or if search index drops >15% over 2-week period

reason_it_will_fire_in_30_days: Healthcare cost stress is cyclical; earnings seasons and policy announcements trigger spikes 4–6 times annually.
